sections.body:
- What is your average client or transaction value? $______________
- If one YouTube video generates just ONE client per year, what is that video worth? $______________
- If that video keeps working for five years (and that's conservative), what is the lifetime value? $______________
- If creating that video takes three hours, what is your "YouTube Hourly Rate"? $______________ per hour

Write your YouTube Hourly Rate on a sticky note and put it somewhere you'll see it every single day.

> "That number is your anchor when creating content feels hard, when you don't want to hit record, when imposter syndrome creeps in. Look at the number. Then press record anyway."

The three YouTube "rules" I've been following or planning to follow:
1. ______________ (Where I learned it: ______________)
2. ______________ (Where I learned it: ______________)
3. ______________ (Where I learned it: ______________)

For each source: Was this from someone who uses YouTube to run a business, or someone who makes money teaching YouTube?

- Average view duration on my last five videos: ______________%
- If under 40%, my content strategy needs work, not my posting frequency. ☐ Acknowledged

The single most important question my ideal client would type into YouTube's search bar:
______________

(That's my next video topic.)

The one myth I'm dropping today:
______________

- Avg CTR > 5% = winning. CTR < 4% = thumbnails/titles bottleneck.
- Avg AVD > 45% = winning. AVD < 35% = hooks/pacing bottleneck.
- Avg length < 8 min = leaving watch time on the table. Length > 15 min with solid retention = sweet spot.

My current bottleneck (circle one):
☐ CTR (people aren't clicking) → fix thumbnails and titles
☐ AVD (people click but leave early) → fix hooks and structure
☐ Length (great retention but videos too short) → make longer videos

The single problem I'm fixing in my next video:
______________

☐ Direct lead generation
☐ Authority building
☐ Digital products
☐ Affiliate revenue
☐ Ad revenue

(Service-based businesses: almost certainly Direct Lead Generation.)

The single most valuable thing I could offer a YouTube viewer for free:
______________

(Format: ☐ Guide ☐ Checklist ☐ Calculator ☐ Free consultation ☐ Market report ☐ Other: ______________)

Tool I'll use: ______________ (Canva / Google Sites / Leadpages / GoHighLevel / Other)
Build deadline: ______________

- Email 1 subject (delivers lead magnet): ______________
- Email 2 subject (additional resource): ______________
- Email 3 subject (soft offer to connect): ______________

- 20 leads/month × my close rate ___ % = ___ new clients/month
- × my average client value $______ = $______ in monthly YouTube revenue
- × 12 = $______ in annual YouTube revenue

(Tape this next to your YouTube Hourly Rate from Chapter 1.)

- Do I own a smartphone made in the last five years? ☐ Yes ☐ No
- Do I own earbuds with a microphone? ☐ Yes ☐ No
- Do I have access to a window with natural light? ☐ Yes ☐ No
- Do I have anything to prop my phone up? ☐ Yes ☐ No

If all four = Yes, you have everything you need to start. Full stop.

The real obstacle (it's almost never equipment):
☐ Don't know what to create (Part Two of book)
☐ Time management (Chapter 14)
☐ Fear of being on camera (Chapter 11)
☐ Other: ______________

- Camera: ______________ (probably your phone)
- Audio: ______________ (probably earbuds or a $25 lav)
- Lighting: ______________ (probably a window)

Specific date: ______________
Person I told for accountability: ______________

Current name: ______________
Passes the radio test? ☐ Yes ☐ No
New name (if needed): ______________

☐ Professional headshot uploaded
☐ Looking at camera, smiling
☐ 800x800 pixels minimum

"Helping [AUDIENCE] achieve [OUTCOME] | [CREDIBILITY MARKER]"
My banner text: ______________
☐ Under 10 words
☐ Tested on phone screen for readability

- Hook with viewer benefit: ______________
- Credibility (2 sentences): ______________
- Content promise + schedule: ______________
- Lead magnet link: ______________
- Booking link: ______________

- "Start Here" playlist with 5 to 10 strongest videos: ☐ Done
- 3 topic-based playlists with searchable names:
  1. ______________
  2. ______________
  3. ______________
- Channel trailer set: ☐ Done
- Featured sections arranged: ☐ Done

Pull up your channel page on your phone. Pretend you've never seen it before. Within 5 seconds, can you answer:
- What is this about? ☐ Yes ☐ No
- Should I trust this person? ☐ Yes ☐ No
- What should I watch? ☐ Yes ☐ No

If any are fuzzy, fix them.

☐ Generic greeting ("Hey everyone, welcome back…")
☐ Subscribe beg
☐ Long intro animation
☐ Slow wind-up
☐ Apology opening

sections.header: Part 2: Best vs Worst by AVD (not by views)
sections.body:
Best AVD video: ______________
- CTR: ______ AVD: ______ Views: ______

Worst AVD video: ______________
- CTR: ______ AVD: ______ Views: ______

What's different (topic? hook? thumbnail? structure? length?): ______________

Pick ONE:
☐ CTR problem (people see but don't click) → fix thumbnails and titles
☐ Hook problem (people click but leave fast) → fix first 30 seconds
☐ Structure problem (people leave mid-video) → fix pacing and pattern interrupts
☐ Impressions problem (not enough people seeing) → consistency is the issue

This is your focus for the next 30 days.

Weekly 15-min review every: ______________ at ______________
☐ Set as recurring calendar appointment

☐ Trailers to drive long-form traffic
☐ Topic tests before committing to full videos
☐ Momentum builders for new channel
☐ Reach extenders for existing content

(Recommendation: 1 Short per 2-3 long-form for business channels)

☐ Standard bridge CTA: ______________

- Track Shorts-to-long-form conversion %
- 90-day decision: if Shorts get views but no long-form traffic or leads, scale back
